title: Foundational AI Safety Papers
summary: A guided note for reading the papers that shaped how AI safety researchers think about alignment, robustness, objectives, and oversight.

notes.heading: Why these papers matter
notes.body: Foundational papers give you the basic problem frames that keep reappearing across AI safety: objective misspecification, distribution shift, scalable oversight, robustness, and alignment failure.

notes.heading: Read for concepts, not completion
notes.body: You do not need to memorize every detail. Focus on the core ideas each paper introduced and how those ideas changed the way researchers talk about AI risk.

notes.heading: Look for recurring patterns
notes.body: Many safety problems rhyme with each other. As you read, watch for repeated themes: proxies breaking under optimization, models behaving differently off-distribution, and oversight becoming harder as systems become more capable.

references.title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
references.note: A clear starting point for framing AI safety as concrete technical problems.

references.title: Scalable Agent Alignment via Reward Modeling
references.note: Introduces reward modeling as one route toward aligning systems when direct supervision becomes difficult.

references.title: Risks from Learned Optimization in Advanced Machine Learning Systems
references.note: A key paper for understanding mesa-optimization and why trained models may develop internal objectives.

author: Shelvia Wongso
